summary: Tested /payments redesign (compact header, inline interest preview, Account Summary card, Statement popup, no auto-INT creation on customer select). All 11 backend tests pass. UI smoke test confirms numbers match across Account Summary, top-right Total Amount Due, and Statement popup subtotals (all show ₱18,423.00 for John Gil Ahig). ELLINE MACAY shows the amber 'No interest rate on file' prompt. Backend confirms charges-preview is read-only and does not create INT invoices on browse.

console_warnings.issue: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot contain a <tr>' / '<span> cannot be a child of <tbody>'. Caused by a wrapper element (data-ve-dynamic / x-source-var=invoices) injected around <tr> rows, likely from a dev-tooling instrumentation. Tables still render correctly but this is a noisy console warning and a real HTML semantic violation. If this wrapper comes from app code (Fragment/conditional rendering returning a span around <tr>s), it should be replaced with React.Fragment.

verification_notes.inline_interest_subrows_currently_zero_f: John Gil already has INT-B1-001004 (₱423) created. Backend charges-preview correctly returns 0 interest_preview rows because last_interest_date is set on the underlying invoices for the current 30-day window. The previous screenshot showing +₱279 + ₱144 = ₱423 inline rows was BEFORE the INT invoice was generated. Sum still adds correctly to ₱18,423 (principal ₱18,000 + existing INT ₱423). Behavior is consistent with spec: inline preview is display-only and the existing INT invoice already represents the prior accrued interest.
verification_notes.save_apply_int_creation_not_executed: Did not actually click Save & Apply with a payment amount in this run (would mutate live DB). Backend contract verified: POST /generate-interest with force=true accepts the request and returns total_interest. Recommend main agent or next iteration verify end-to-end Save & Apply -> INT creation manually.

action_items: (LOW) Investigate React hydration warning '<span> cannot contain <tr>' on PaymentsPage invoice table. Likely a wrapper around invoice rows (tr) needs to be a React.Fragment instead of a <span>.
critical_code_review_comments: PaymentsPage.js renders some wrapper (span) around <tr> elements inside <tbody>, producing React hydration warnings. Should use <React.Fragment> for any conditional/dynamic group of <tr>s.
/api/customers/{id}/charges-preview correctly excludes interest_charge/penalty_charge from the principal/interest computation (line 1738) and does not write to DB — proper read-only design.
/api/customers/{id}/generate-interest 30-day guard (line 1404) is sound; force=true override is the documented path used by Save & Apply. Code matches spec.
/api/customers/{id}/invoices uses {_id: 0} projection (line 1367) — no ObjectId leakage. Verified.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all UX flows pass; one console hydration warning)

seed_data_creation: None. Tests read-only on customer/invoice endpoints and use force=true generate-interest only with idempotent contract assertions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_payments_redesign_162.py covers invoices endpoint, charges-preview endpoint (no _id leakage, correct shape, no DB writes), and generate-interest endpoint (30-day guard + force override). For full end-to-end Save & Apply -> INT invoice creation, manually click Save & Apply on John Gil Ahig with a payment amount > 0 and verify mongo db.invoices.count_documents({customer_id: '0ff5cf41-93b6-4df1-a014-4c674263b8cf', sale_type: 'interest_charge'}) increases. The current setup already has INT-B1-001004 created within 30 days, so without force, generate-interest will be skipped — force=true (used by Save & Apply path in PaymentsPage) is required.
